title: The length returned from a stream read should be checked
description: The length returned from a stream read should be checkedAnalyze your codeintentionality-completereliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoInvoking a stream reading method without verifying the number of bytes read can lead to erroneous assumptions. A Stream can represent any I/O operation, such as reading a file, network communication, or inter-process communication. As such, it is not guaranteed that thebyte[]passed into the method will be filled with the requested number of bytes.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: The length returned from a stream Read/ReadAsync should be checked - the buffer may not be fully filled

logic.detection_conditions: Stream.Read or Stream.ReadAsync call without checking the return value
logic.triggering_conditions: source contains stream.Read() or stream.ReadAsync() call as statement without assignment to check return value
exclusion_conditions: Read call with return value captured (int bytesRead = ...)
never_trigger_on: Read calls where return value is assigned to a variable
Read calls followed by bytesRead check
logic.reasoning: Stream.Read() and ReadAsync() may not fill the entire buffer - they return the actual number of bytes read. Not checking this return value can lead to processing uninitialized or stale data in the buffer.
